leagueBriefing: The Big Dance officially tipped off with the First Four, as Nebraska and Missouri State secured their spots in the main bracket with convincing double-digit victories in their respective regions.

games.headline: Nebraska stifles Richmond to punch ticket to Sacramento
games.brief: Nebraska left little doubt in their First Four matchup, using a dominant defensive performance and a balanced scoring attack to overwhelm Richmond. The Huskers controlled the glass and shut down the Spiders' perimeter game early, building a double-digit lead that Richmond simply couldn't chip away at. It was a statement win for a Nebraska team looking to prove they belong deep in the tournament field.
bullets: Nebraska outscored Richmond 22-10 in the second quarter to break the game open.
The Huskers' bench contributed 24 points, providing a massive spark in the middle periods.
Richmond was held to just 34% shooting from the field and struggled with Nebraska's length.
Alexis Markowski anchored the paint for Nebraska, finishing with a double-double.
The win marks Nebraska's first tournament victory since 2014.
games.context: Nebraska advances as the 11-seed in Regional 2 to face 6-seeded North Carolina.

games.headline: Missouri State outlasts Stephen F. Austin in high-scoring First Four battle
games.brief: In a fast-paced shootout in Fort Worth, Missouri State's efficiency at the free-throw line and clinical fast-break execution proved to be the difference against Stephen F. Austin. The Lady Bears weathered an early storm from the Ladyjacks, who leaned heavily on the three-ball, but Missouri State's interior presence eventually wore down SFA. It was a classic tournament battle where the more disciplined team survived the chaotic pace.
bullets: Missouri State shot an impressive 88% from the free-throw line (22-of-25).
Stephen F. Austin connected on 11 three-pointers but struggled to get stops late in the fourth quarter.
The Lady Bears recorded 18 second-chance points off 14 offensive rebounds.
Missouri State's backcourt combined for 45 of the team's 85 points.
This victory snaps a three-game tournament losing streak for the Lady Bears.
games.context: Missouri State moves on to face 1-seeded South Carolina in the Regional 3 bracket.
